title: A Changing Road: European Carmakers and the Rise of Chinese Rivals
subtitle: As European car manufacturers face falling sales, they are increasingly turning to Chinese companies for partnerships and factory space.

summary.en: European car manufacturers are currently facing a difficult period with lower sales and extra factory space. At the same time, Chinese car companies are expanding rapidly across the European market. To solve their financial problems, some European brands are allowing Chinese companies to use their factories. While this helps avoid closing plants and firing workers, it also creates new competition. Industry leaders remain divided on whether these partnerships will help or hurt European businesses in the long term.
summary.zh: 歐洲汽車製造商目前面臨銷售下滑與工廠閒置的艱難時期。與此同時，中國車廠正迅速擴張歐洲市場。為了緩解財務問題，部分歐洲品牌允許中國企業使用其工廠。雖然這有助於避免關廠與裁員，但也帶來了新的競爭。業界領袖對於這些合作關係長期而言究竟是有助於還是傷害歐洲企業，意見仍分歧。

paragraphs.en: The global car industry is currently experiencing a major shift in power. For many years, European companies led the market, but today, Chinese carmakers are growing quickly and gaining influence. This change is becoming very clear as Chinese brands look for ways to build their vehicles directly inside Europe, while traditional European manufacturers struggle with lower sales and empty factory space.
paragraphs.zh: 全球汽車產業目前正經歷一場重大的權力轉移。多年來，歐洲企業一直引領市場，但如今中國車廠正迅速成長並擴大影響力。這種變化正變得非常明顯，因為中國品牌正設法直接在歐洲境內製造車輛，而傳統的歐洲製造商則面臨銷售下滑與工廠閒置的困境。

paragraphs.en: According to The Guardian Business, Chinese car sales in western Europe have grown significantly. In the first three months of the year, these vehicles made up 8.6% of the market, which is nearly double the amount from the same time last year. Matthias Schmidt, an automotive analyst based in Berlin, noted this rapid increase as a sign of the changing landscape. As these Chinese companies, such as BYD, Geely, and Xpeng, look to expand further, they are now searching for places to manufacture their cars within Europe.
paragraphs.zh: 根據《衛報》商業版報導，中國汽車在西歐的銷售量已顯著成長。今年前三個月，這些車輛佔據了市場百分之八點六的份額，幾乎是去年同期的兩倍。駐柏林的汽車分析師馬蒂亞斯·施密特（Matthias Schmidt）指出，這種快速增長是市場版圖變遷的跡象。隨著比亞迪（BYD）、吉利（Geely）與小鵬（Xpeng）等中國企業尋求進一步擴張，他們現在正積極在歐洲尋找製造汽車的地點。

paragraphs.en: For European carmakers, this situation creates a difficult choice. Many companies have seen their total sales drop from 15.3 million in 2019 to less than 13 million in 2025. This decrease, combined with new US tariffs — taxes on goods brought into a country — has made it harder for European brands to sell their cars abroad. As a result, these companies have more factory space than they actually need. Instead of closing these sites and firing thousands of workers, some European manufacturers are choosing to rent or sell their extra space to their new Chinese competitors.
paragraphs.zh: 對於歐洲車廠而言，這種情況造成了兩難的抉擇。許多公司的總銷售量已從二〇一九年的一千五百三十萬輛，降至二〇二五年的一千三百萬輛以下。這種衰退加上美國新實施的關稅（即對進口商品徵收的稅款），使得歐洲品牌更難將汽車銷往海外。結果，這些公司擁有的工廠空間遠超過實際需求。為了避免關閉廠房並解僱數千名員工，部分歐洲製造商選擇將多餘的空間出租或出售給新的中國競爭對手。

paragraphs.en: This trend is already visible across the continent. For example, Nissan is currently in talks with the Chinese brand Chery to share part of its factory in northern England. Meanwhile, Ford has reportedly agreed to sell a portion of its plant in Spain to Geely. Stellantis, which owns brands like Fiat and Peugeot, has also started working with a Chinese partner called Leapmotor to build cars in its Spanish facilities. Antonio Filosa, the chief executive of Stellantis, stated that he believes a strong partnership can be beneficial for both sides.
paragraphs.zh: 這種趨勢在歐洲大陸已隨處可見。例如，日產（Nissan）目前正與中國品牌奇瑞（Chery）洽談，計畫共享其位於英格蘭北部的工廠。同時，據報導福特（Ford）已同意將其西班牙廠房的一部分出售給吉利。擁有飛雅特（Fiat）與寶獅（Peugeot）等品牌的斯泰蘭蒂斯（Stellantis），也已開始與中國合作夥伴零跑汽車（Leapmotor）合作，在西班牙的設施中生產汽車。斯泰蘭蒂斯執行長安東尼奧·費洛薩（Antonio Filosa）表示，他相信強大的合作關係對雙方皆有利。

paragraphs.en: However, not every deal is easy to make. Elvis Cheng, a director at Xpeng, recently shared his thoughts on working with European partners. He noted that some of the older factories currently available in Europe are not suitable for modern car production. While Xpeng is still interested in finding a location in Europe, the company is also considering building its own new factories from the ground up. Similarly, Thomas Schäfer, the chief executive of the Volkswagen brand, mentioned that finding the right buyer for older factories is not always simple, noting that there is not always a line of companies waiting to take over these sites.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，並非每一筆交易都容易達成。小鵬汽車的總監鄭先生（Elvis Cheng）近期分享了他對與歐洲夥伴合作的看法。他指出，目前歐洲部分老舊工廠並不適合現代化的汽車生產。雖然小鵬仍有興趣在歐洲尋找據點，但該公司也在考慮從零開始興建自己的新工廠。同樣地，福斯（Volkswagen）品牌執行長湯瑪斯·謝弗（Thomas Schäfer）提到，為老舊工廠找到合適的買家並不總是那麼簡單，並指出並非隨時都有排隊的企業等著接手這些廠房。

paragraphs.en: Behind the scenes, the mood among European car executives is more cautious. One executive at a large manufacturer told The Guardian Business that Chinese companies are now very credible and represent a serious threat to traditional brands. This competition is not limited to cheap, mass-market cars; it also reaches into the luxury car market. Despite these private concerns, many European bosses continue to say publicly that these partnerships are a necessary step to stay competitive in a changing global economy.
paragraphs.zh: 在檯面下，歐洲汽車高層的心態更為謹慎。一位大型製造商的高層告訴《衛報》商業版，中國企業現在已具備高度的公信力，對傳統品牌構成了嚴重的威脅。這種競爭不僅限於廉價的大眾市場車款，更延伸至豪華車市場。儘管有這些私下的擔憂，許多歐洲企業主管在公開場合仍持續表示，這些合作關係是為了在變動的全球經濟中保持競爭力所必須採取的步驟。

paragraphs.en: Looking ahead, the future of the European car industry remains uncertain. While selling factory space provides immediate cash and protects jobs in the short term, it also helps Chinese rivals establish a stronger presence on European soil. Some companies are trying to balance this by looking for partners outside of China as well, hoping to keep their options open. As the market continues to evolve, European manufacturers must decide if these partnerships are a smart way to survive or if they are simply helping their rivals grow faster at their own expense. For now, the door remains open, and the shift in the global car market shows no signs of slowing down.
paragraphs.zh: 展望未來，歐洲汽車產業的前景依然充滿不確定性。雖然出售工廠空間能提供即時現金並在短期內保障就業，但這也幫助了中國競爭對手在歐洲土地上建立更穩固的立足點。一些公司正試圖透過尋找中國以外的合作夥伴來平衡這種風險，希望能保留更多選擇。隨著市場持續演變，歐洲製造商必須決定這些合作關係究竟是生存的明智之舉，還是僅僅在犧牲自身利益的同時，加速了對手的成長。目前看來，大門依然敞開，全球汽車市場的變革絲毫沒有放緩的跡象。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To constitute or form a certain amount or proportion of a whole.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 構成、組成（某個比例或總數）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「編造（故事）」，這裡指佔據市場份額。文中：In the first three months of the year, these vehicles made up 8.6% of the market, which is nearly double the amount from the same time last year.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Starting from the very beginning, completely new.
keyTerms.noteZh: 字面意思容易誤解，這裡指從無到有地建設工廠。文中：While Xpeng is still interested in finding a location in Europe, the company is also considering building its own new factories from the ground up.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A number of people or things arranged in a row.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「線條」，這裡指等待的人或企業隊伍。文中：Thomas Schäfer, the chief executive of the Volkswagen brand, mentioned that finding the right buyer for older factories is not always simple, noting that there is not always a line of companies waiting to take over these sites.

keyTerms.definitionEn: In a way that causes harm or loss to oneself.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「自費」，這裡指損害自身利益。文中：As the market continues to evolve, European manufacturers must decide if these partnerships are a smart way to survive or if they are simply helping their rivals grow faster at their own expense.

sources.name: The Guardian Business
sources.title: EU carmakers pave way for Chinese rivals as balance in market shifts

quiz.question: What is one specific reason mentioned for the decline in European car manufacturers' sales performance?

options.A: A sudden increase in the cost of raw materials for car production.
options.B: The implementation of new taxes on goods exported to the United States.
options.C: A lack of interest from European consumers in buying local car brands.
options.D: The decision to close all factories in the northern region of England.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第三段，歐洲車廠銷量下滑的原因之一是「new US tariffs」（對進口到美國的商品徵收的關稅），這使得歐洲品牌更難在國外銷售汽車。選項 A 文章未提及；選項 C 文章並未指出消費者對本土品牌失去興趣；選項 D 為錯誤資訊，Nissan 只是在談判租借廠房，而非全面關閉。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the attitude of European car executives toward Chinese competitors?

options.A: They believe Chinese companies will soon go bankrupt due to high production costs.
options.B: They are confident that European technology will always remain superior to Chinese technology.
options.C: They feel pressured by the strength of Chinese brands but publicly maintain a cooperative stance.
options.D: They are actively trying to block Chinese companies from entering the European market.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據第六段，雖然歐洲車廠主管在私下承認中國公司是「嚴重的威脅」（serious threat），但他們在公開場合仍表示這些合作是保持競爭力的必要步驟。這顯示了私下擔憂與公開合作之間的矛盾。選項 A、B 文章未提及；選項 D 與文章中「選擇出租廠房給競爭對手」的事實衝突。

quiz.question: In the fifth paragraph, what does the phrase 'from the ground up' mean in the context of building factories?

options.A: Renovating existing, older factory buildings to meet modern standards.
options.B: Starting the entire construction process from the very beginning.
options.C: Focusing only on the foundation and floor structure of the factory.
options.D: Buying land that was previously used for agricultural purposes.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。片語「from the ground up」在此處意指從零開始、從基礎做起。文章提到 Xpeng 認為舊工廠不適合現代生產，因此考慮「building its own new factories from the ground up」，意即建造全新的工廠。選項 A 與文意相反；選項 C 過於狹隘；選項 D 並非該片語的定義。

quiz.question: What is the central theme of the article?

options.A: The history of how European car companies dominated the global market for decades.
options.B: The technical challenges Chinese companies face when trying to build cars in Europe.
options.C: The shift in power in the car industry and how European manufacturers are responding to Chinese competition.
options.D: The environmental benefits of sharing factory space between different international car brands.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章全文圍繞著全球汽車產業勢力消長，描述中國車廠崛起，以及歐洲車廠在銷量下滑的壓力下，被迫透過出租廠房或合作來應對競爭的複雜處境。選項 A 僅是背景介紹；選項 B 只是其中一個小細節；選項 D 文章完全未探討環保議題。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
